message: Run created: add, update, remove doc and readme to align with software industry standdard and AI coding agent and introduce this product and guid new mem to join project
Task started: Review and Analyze Existing Documentation and README

details.text: Now let me verify the actual project structure against what's documented.
Now I have a full picture. Let me create the documentation update plan.
**Analysis complete.** Created `docs/documentation-update-plan.md` with 33 identified issues across 5 documents, categorized into 3 priority phases.
| **AI agent duplication** | README's AI Agent section largely duplicates `docs/ai-agent.md`; neither references `.flowtask/` workflow. |

| **Structure mismatches** | Both `product-overview.md` and `contribution-guide.md` show a fictional `features/` at the project root — features actually live under `apps/web/src/features/`. Both also omit `packages/settings` and `packages/ai-tutor`. |
| **user-guide.md severely incomplete** | Only covers 2 features (Getting Started, AI Progress Review) out of 17+. |
| **Missing onboarding** | No "new member" or "new contributor" section exists anywhere. No community links, good-first-issue pointers, or communication channels. |
| **Stale docs clutter** | ~30 working/audit docs in `docs/` with no separation from stable reference docs. |
**Priority actions:** Fix project structure trees → Add new contributor onboarding → Expand user-guide → Add missing packages everywhere → Condense README AI section.

message: Task started: Update docs/product-overview.md to Align with Industry Standards and Introduce AI Coding Agent

details.text: Let me explore the project structure and existing docs for context.

+> A local-first, offline-capable IELTS preparation system that runs entirely in the browser with no backend required. Built with Clean Architecture, strict TypeScript, and designed to be AI coding agent-friendly.

+IELTS Learning Journey is a comprehensive study tool for IELTS exam preparation. It helps learners manage vocabulary, practice all four IELTS skills (Reading, Listening, Writing, Speaking), track mistakes, monitor progress, and optionally use AI for feedback — all while keeping data private and local. The codebase is also designed to be AI coding agent-friendly, enabling automated code exploration and safe, predictable modifications.
-> A local-first, offline-capable IELTS preparation system that runs entirely in the browser with no backend required.

-IELTS Learning Journey is a comprehensive study tool for IELTS exam preparation. It helps learners manage vocabulary, practice all four IELTS skills (Reading, Listening, Writing, Speaking), track mistakes, monitor progress, and optionally use AI for feedback — all while keeping data private and local.
- **Local-first**: All data lives in your browser. You own it.
Now I have the full context. I'll add an AI Coding Agent Integration section (section 10) and shift Getting Started to section 11.
+### Why AI Agents Work Well Here
+This codebase is designed to be **AI coding agent-friendly**. The following design decisions make the code predictable, statically analyzable, and safe to modify through automated tooling.
+| **Clean Architecture** | Strict layer separation makes it easy to identify where code belongs |
+| **Strict TypeScript** | Full type information enables safe refactoring and accurate code generation |
+| **Repository Pattern** | Data access follows a predictable, consistent interface |
+| **Feature Modules** | New features can mirror existing patterns exactly |
+| **Pure Domain Logic** | Domain packages have zero framework dependencies, making them easy to test and modify |
+| **Zod Validation** | Every data boundary is explicitly defined and validated |
+### How AI Agents Should Approach This Codebase
+1. **Start with the architecture** — Read this document and [architecture.md](architecture.md) for layer separation understanding
+| **Pre-built CodeGraph Index** | Symbol search, caller/callee analysis, and blast radius computation available out of the box |
+3. **Follow the data flow** — `Component → Hook → Service → Repository → IndexedDB`. Never call the database directly from UI
+6. **Respect layer isolation** — Domain packages must not import from Presentation or Infrastructure layers
+4. **Mirror existing patterns** — New features should follow the same structure as existing feature modules
+5. **Validate with Zod** — Every data boundary requires a Zod schema
+2. **Use CodeGraph** — Run `codegraph explore "<question>"` for structural answers instead of grepping files
+- **Symbol Search** — Find any symbol by name
+- **Caller/Cal** — List every call site of a function, including callback registrations
+- **Blast Radius** — Determine what a change would affect
+- **File Reading** — Read files with line numbers (index-served, faster than direct I/O)
+2. Check [contribution-guide.md](contribution-guide.md) for conventions and PR process
+3. Explore the [docs/](docs/) directory for detailed documentation on each subsystem
+1. Read this overview and [architecture.md](architecture.md) to understand the system
+4. Run `pnpm test` and `pnpm lint` before submitting changes
See [deployment.md](deployment.md) for build and deployment instructions.

Before considering a change complete, an AI agent should verify:
- [ ] New features follow the feature module pattern
- [ ] Data boundaries are validated with Zod schemas
@@ -342,6 +545,53 @@ Before considering a change complete, an AI agent should verify:
- [ ] No direct IndexedDB calls from components
+- [ ] Relevant `docs/` files are updated if behavior changed
+- [ ] New exports are added to barrel (`index.ts`) files
+- [ ] Public API changes are reflected in documentation
+- [ ] Edge cases are considered (empty state, error state, loading state)
+- **Always read first** — Understand the code before editing it. CodeGraph makes this fast.
+- **Check the docs** — Documentation in `docs/` is the source of truth for architecture and conventions.
+- **Mirror, don't invent** — If adding a new feature, copy the structure and naming of an existing feature.
+- **Use types as guardrails** — Strict TypeScript will catch many mistakes at compile time. Let it.
+- **Know the blast radius** — Before changing a function, check its callers. A small change can break many consumers.
+- **One thing at a time** — Make one logical change, validate it, then move to the next.
+- **Keep changes minimal** — Prefer surgical edits over rewrites. Smaller diffs are safer and easier to review.
+- **Check with `git diff`** — Review your changes before reporting completion to catch unintended modifications.
+- **Run the full validation suite** — Do not rely on a single check. Run typecheck, lint, and tests.
+- **Run validation early** — Run `typecheck` and `test` before making changes to confirm a healthy baseline.
+- **Report clearly** — Tell the user what changed, why, and what validation passed.
+| Editing files before understanding their role | Use `codegraph explore` first |
+| Making large multi-file changes at once | Break work into small, verifiable steps |
+| Ignoring the blast radius | Always check callers before modifying public functions |
+| Skipping validation between changes | Run `pnpm typecheck` after each logical step |
+| Adding dependencies without asking | Propose the dependency and explain why it is needed |
+| Modifying test expectations instead of fixing the real issue | Read the test to understand expected behavior |

Now I have a thorough understanding of all AI features in the codebase. Let me rewrite the user guide with a proper welcome section, onboarding flow, dedicated AI configuration, and coverage of all AI features.
+> Welcome to IELTS Learning Journey! This guide walks you through getting started, using core and AI features, and getting the most out of your study experience.
-> How to use IELTS Journey's features to maximize your study efficiency.
-Open IELTS Journey in your browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari). All features work after installation — no sign-up required.

+IELTS Learning Journey is a comprehensive, local-first IELTS preparation tool that runs entirely in your browser. It helps you manage vocabulary, practice all four IELTS skills (Reading, Listening, Writing, Speaking), track mistakes, monitor progress, and optionally use AI for personalized feedback — all while keeping your data private and under your control.
-- **AI features** (AI Learning Progress Review, AI Tutor): Require an OpenAI-compatible API key configured in Settings
-- **Core features** (vocabulary, practice logging, analytics): Work offline immediately
+- **Privacy-safe**: No cloud, no telemetry, no data leaving your device unless you explicitly use AI features with your own API key.
+- **AI-enhanced**: Optional AI integration provides personalized tutoring, progress reviews, exercise generation, and more.
+- **Local-first**: All data lives in your browser. You own it.
-To configure AI, go to **Settings → AI Provider** and enter your API key, model, and endpoint. The default model is `gpt-4o-mini`.
+- **Offline-capable**: Core features work without internet. AI features require a connection but are entirely optional.
+- **Chrome users**: Use the extension for collecting content from any webpage directly into your study database.
+3. **Feature tour** — A quick walkthrough of the main sections
+1. **Welcome screen** — A brief introduction to the app
+The first time you open the app, an onboarding wizard will guide you through:
+4. **AI configuration prompt** — Optionally configure AI (or skip and do it later from Settings)
+2. **Profile setup** — Set your target band score and exam date (optional but recommended)
+- **Install as PWA**: Click the install icon in your browser's address bar to add IELTS Learning Journey to your home screen. It will then work like a native app and remain usable offline.
+You can revisit the onboarding at any time from **Settings → Onboarding**.
+| **Vocabulary Management** | Add, tag, filter, and review words with spaced repetition |
+| **Listening Practice** | Log listening sessions, track scores |
+| **Reading Practice** | Log reading sessions, track comprehension |
+| **Speaking Practice** | Log speaking sessions across Parts 1–3 |
+| **Writing Practice** | Practice Task 1 & 2 essays, track band estimates |
+| **Grammar Notes** | Organize grammar topics with examples |
+Open IELTS Learning Journey in any modern browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari). For the best experience:
+These features work immediately after opening the app — no sign-up, no API key, and no internet required:
- **Getting Started** — Expanded from 3 paragraphs to 3 sub-sections covering opening the app, first-run onboarding wizard, and a feature table distinguishing core (no setup) vs AI features
The user guide has been rewritten with the following improvements:
- **AI Configuration** — Dedicated section with prerequisite, step-by-step setup, and key management guidance
- **AI Learning Progress Review** — Preserved existing high-quality content, reorganized with clearer subsection numbering
- **Welcome section** — Introduces the product and its core philosophy to new users
- **Other AI Features** — Brief coverage of vocabulary enrichment, article/video analysis, and browser extension
- **Troubleshooting** — New section with table-formatted solutions for AI, data, and general issues
Acceptance criteria met: clear getting started for new users, easy-to-follow AI usage instructions, well-structured content free of outdated information.
- **Tips & Best Practices** — Categorized advice for all users, AI users, and privacy
- **Proactive Messages** — New section covering trigger types, display surfaces, and notification customization
- **AI Tutor Chat** — New section covering 11 assistant modes, context awareness, customization options, and usage examples
- **Exercise Generator** — New section covering vocabulary-based, content-based, and chat-based exercise generation

message: Task started: Update README.md to Introduce Product and Guide New Members

details.text: +Welcome! This section helps you get oriented and start contributing quickly.
+1. **Read the docs** — Start with [Product Overview](docs/product-overview.md) and [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) to understand the system
+3. **Explore the codebase** — Use `codegraph explore "<question>"` to navigate symbols and understand dependencies
+4. **Find a starting issue** — Look for `good first issue` or `help wanted` labels on GitHub
+5. **Review the [Contribution Guide](docs/contribution-guide.md)** — Read conventions, PR process, and coding standards
+- **GitHub Issues** — Report bugs and request features
+- **GitHub Discussions** — Ask questions and suggest ideas
-All user data is stored **client-side only** — no backend, no cloud sync, no telemetry:
-| **localStorage** | Settings (theme, target band, AI API key, preferences) |
-| **IndexedDB** | Vocabulary, study sessions, logs, grammar notes, mistakes, progress, chat history |
-Component → Custom Hook → Feature Service → Repository → IndexedDB
The Chrome extension (Manifest V3) allows collecting learning material from any webpage:
Now I'll make targeted edits to the README: add an onboarding section, remove redundant Data Architecture, merge Testing/Linting, and update Contributing.
+Run these checks regularly — all must pass before merging:
pnpm test:unit     # Run unit tests with Vitest
-- React Testing Library for component tests
+**Testing**: Vitest with `fake-indexeddb` (repository tests), React Testing Library (component tests), `jsdom` (browser simulation), and per-package configs in `packages/*/vitest.config.ts`.
+**Linting**: ESLint configured for consistent code style.
-Both commands must pass before merging. Run them as part of your development workflow.
Now I'll merge Testing and Linting, and update Contributing to reference onboarding:
